name: Chocoholics Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the chocolate in the oven for 10 to 12 minutes, or until it develops a toasty aroma. Remove from the oven and set aside to cool.
2. Increase the oven temperature to 350°F (175°C).
3. Place the unsweetened chocolate, 12 ounces semi-sweet chocolate, butter, margarine, and espresso in the top of a double boiler over simmering (not boiling) water until the chocolate is about three-quarters melted.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat the eggs with a wire whisk. Gradually add sugar, whisking until the mixture becomes thick and light in color.
5. When the chocolate is almost melted, remove from heat and stir until completely melted and satiny in appearance.
6. Gradually whisk the chocolate into the egg mixture.
7. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt directly into the chocolate mixture. Gently stir until the dry ingredients are barely incorporated.
8. Cut the 6 ounces of semi-sweet chocolate into ½-inch chunks and stir into the dough along with the toasted nuts.
9. Lightly butter four aluminum baking sheets (do not use foil or cookies will burn).
10. Drop dough by tablespoonfuls, about 12 to a sheet, leaving room for cookies to spread.
11. Bake one sheet at a time in the center of the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the cookies lose their shine. They will still be very soft.
12. Allow cookies to cool for 2 minutes before removing from pans.
13. Using a pin-sized tip on an icing bag, pipe melted semi-sweet chocolate onto cookies in a criss-cross pattern. Allow to cool at least 1 hour before storing in tins. Makes 50 one-ounce cookies
